project.name: ClaudetRelay
project.description: Multi-agent AI workspace for Windows — run up to 20 AI participants from multiple providers (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Mistral, Groq, OpenRouter, xAI Grok, Ollama) in a single conversation.

nodes.name: decisions.md
nodes.summary: Template decision log for Business Project instances, providing a structured record of architectural and strategic choices with date, alternatives, and reasoning fields.

nodes.name: project_brief.md
nodes.summary: Template project brief for Business Project instances, capturing objectives, stakeholders, success criteria, constraints, and out-of-scope items as placeholder sections.

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Template task list for Business Project instances, organized into Backlog, In Progress, and Done columns following a lightweight kanban structure.

nodes.name: characters.md
nodes.summary: Template document for defining characters and NPCs in a Game Project type, providing a structured placeholder section for per-character descriptions that AI participants can populate.

nodes.name: game_design.md
nodes.summary: Core Game Design Document template for the Game Project type, covering concept, design pillars, core mechanics, win/lose conditions, progression, art direction, and out-of-scope items via placeholder headings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses {{ProjectName}} mustache-style placeholders, indicating these templates are filled in at project creation time within ClaudetRelay.

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Task tracking template for the Game Project type, providing a simple Kanban-style structure with Backlog, In Progress, and Done sections for managing development work items.

nodes.name: book_outline.md
nodes.summary: Template document for structuring a non-fiction book project, providing placeholder sections for central thesis, target audience, chapter structure table, and key sources.

nodes.name: key_arguments.md
nodes.summary: Template document for capturing the main thesis, supporting arguments, counter-arguments with responses, and an evidence map for a non-fiction book project.

nodes.name: research_notes.md
nodes.summary: Template document for collecting research sources, key findings, quotes, and page references to support non-fiction book writing.

nodes.name: characters.md
nodes.summary: Template document for defining characters in a Novel project, providing structured fields for role, background, goals, flaws, arc, and voice. Serves as a reusable scaffold populated by ClaudetRelay when a new Novel project is created.

nodes.name: locations.md
nodes.summary: Template document for cataloguing story locations in a Novel project, with fields for type, description, atmosphere, story significance, and first appearance. Acts as a companion scaffold to characters.md within the Novel project plan.

nodes.name: plot_notes.md
nodes.summary: Freeform scratch-pad template for tracking open plot questions, unresolved threads, continuity notes, and discarded ideas in a Novel project. Provides lightweight structure for iterative story development.

nodes.name: story_outline.md
nodes.summary: Master story outline template covering premise, themes, genre, point-of-view, three-act narrative structure, and a chapter list table. Acts as the top-level planning document for a Novel project within ClaudetRelay.

nodes.name: campaign_overview.md
nodes.summary: Template document for a tabletop RPG campaign overview, covering premise, themes, tone, world summary, central conflict, campaign arc structure, and session list — all pre-filled with instructional placeholder text for the GM to complete.

nodes.name: encounters.md
nodes.summary: Template document for planning individual encounters and set pieces in a tabletop RPG campaign, with structured fields for encounter type, difficulty, setup, objectives, key actors, twists, rewards, and GM notes.

nodes.name: npcs.md
nodes.summary: Template document for tracking non-player characters in a tabletop RPG campaign, including fields for role, faction, motivation, secrets, attitude toward the party, and a summary NPC tracker table.

nodes.name: party.md
nodes.summary: Template document for recording player character details in a tabletop RPG campaign, with fields for race, class, level, background, goals, personal arcs, and sections for party dynamics and shared resources.

nodes.name: plot_notes.md
nodes.summary: Freeform scratch-pad template for a tabletop RPG campaign's ongoing story management, organizing open questions, active plot threads, foreshadowing seeds, continuity notes, and discarded ideas.

nodes.name: session_log.md
nodes.summary: Template document for logging individual tabletop RPG sessions, capturing events, key decisions, NPCs encountered, loot, plot hooks introduced, and post-session GM notes.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Template document for capturing software architecture details, including system overview, tech stack table, key components, external dependencies, and data flow. All content is placeholder and not yet filled in.

nodes.name: decisions.md
nodes.summary: Template decision log for recording significant design choices and their rationale, including fields for decision title, date, alternatives considered, reasoning, and consequences. No entries have been filled in yet.

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Template task tracking document with Backlog, In Progress, and Done sections using GitHub-style checkboxes. Only a placeholder 'Project created' entry exists in the Done section.

nodes.name: characters.md
nodes.summary: Template document for defining characters in a Theatre project plan, providing a structured placeholder for character names and descriptions to be filled in per production.

nodes.name: play_structure.md
nodes.summary: Template document outlining the structural framework of a Theatre project, covering synopsis, setting, act/scene breakdown, and themes as sections to be populated per production.

nodes.name: stage_notes.md
nodes.summary: Template document for recording staging conventions, recurring stage directions, prop lists, and costume notes for a Theatre project plan.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git attributes file enforcing consistent line endings for the repository.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion list for the understand-anything analysis tool, specifying paths and patterns to skip during codebase scanning.
nodes.languageNotes: 230 non-empty lines of ignore patterns, similar in structure to .gitignore.

nodes.name: App.xaml
nodes.summary: WPF application XAML defining global resource dictionaries, merged theme dictionaries, and application-level styles used throughout ClaudetRelay.
nodes.languageNotes: XAML Application element with merged ResourceDictionary entries wiring in theming and global styles.

nodes.name: App.xaml.cs
nodes.summary: WPF application entry point that validates Windows version and .NET runtime dependencies on startup, displaying a styled dependency error dialog if requirements are not met.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: WPF Application subclass handling startup validation for OS and runtime prerequisites before opening the main window.

nodes.name: OnStartup
nodes.summary: Overrides WPF OnStartup to check Windows 11 and .NET 10 prerequisites, shutting down with an error dialog if either is missing.

nodes.name: ShowDependencyError
nodes.summary: Programmatically constructs and displays a styled WPF dialog showing a dependency error with title, detail text, and a download link.

nodes.name: AssemblyInfo.cs
nodes.summary: Assembly metadata file setting WPF theme dictionary location and compatibility attributes for the ClaudetRelay assembly.

nodes.name: ClaudetRelay.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the ClaudetRelay WPF application targeting .NET 10 on Windows, declaring NuGet dependencies including HTTP, JSON, and AI SDK packages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SDK-style csproj format with UseWPF=true and explicit PackageReference entries for all AI provider SDKs.

nodes.name: ClaudetRelay.slnx
nodes.summary: Visual Studio solution file in the newer .slnx format referencing the ClaudetRelay project.

nodes.name: MainWindow.World.cs
nodes.summary: Partial class of MainWindow implementing the World Builder feature: entity management (characters, locations, factions), board gallery, and all associated dialogs for creating and editing world entities.
nodes.languageNotes: C# partial class pattern splitting MainWindow across multiple files; this file owns all World Builder UI construction methods.

nodes.name: MainWindow
nodes.summary: Partial MainWindow implementation providing world entity listing, board gallery, entity editing dialogs, and faction visualization for the World Builder panel.

nodes.name: BuildWorldContent
nodes.summary: Dynamically constructs the entire World Builder UI panel including entity-type tabs, filter controls, entity cards, and the boards gallery tab.

nodes.name: BuildEntityCard
nodes.summary: Constructs a rich WPF card UI element for a single world entity, displaying portrait, faction affiliation, relationships, and action buttons.

nodes.name: BuildWorldBoardGallery
nodes.summary: Builds the board gallery panel for the World Builder, showing all project boards as clickable cards with preview images and management actions.

nodes.name: ShowNewBoardDialog
nodes.summary: Presents a dialog for creating a new world board, allowing the user to set name, size, background image, and grid settings.

nodes.name: ShowBoardSettingsDialog
nodes.summary: Presents a dialog for editing an existing board's settings including name, dimensions, grid, and background.

nodes.name: ShowCharacterPickerDialog
nodes.summary: Shows a scrollable dialog allowing the user to pick a character entity from the world, filtering out already-selected IDs.

nodes.name: ShowSimpleInputDialog
nodes.summary: Generic single-field text input dialog used throughout the World Builder for naming operations.

nodes.name: ShowEntityReadOnlyDialog
nodes.summary: Displays a read-only view dialog for a world entity showing all fields in a non-editable scrollable layout.

nodes.name: BuildWorldEntityContext
nodes.summary: Assembles a text context block describing all world entities for injection into AI system prompts.

nodes.name: MainWindow.xaml
nodes.summary: XAML layout definition for the main application window, declaring the top-level grid structure with navigation tabs, chat panel, project panel, bridge panel, and sidebar controls.
nodes.languageNotes: 807-line XAML with named elements referenced by code-behind for dynamic UI construction.

nodes.name: MainWindow.xaml.cs
nodes.summary: Primary code-behind for the main application window (~18,800 lines), implementing multi-agent chat orchestration, project management, roadmap, file manager, bridge/MCP server mode, theming, AI provider integration, and all major UI interaction logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Massive WPF partial class (~278 methods) acting as the central controller for the entire application; bridges all AI provider APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral, Groq, OpenRouter, xAI, Ollama) through a unified streaming interface.

nodes.name: MainWindow
nodes.summary: Core WPF Window class orchestrating multi-agent AI chat, project lifecycle, roadmap management, MCP bridge mode, theming, and all AI provider communication for ClaudetRelay.

nodes.name: InitializeServices
nodes.summary: Initializes all application services including HTTP client, MCP server, status timer, bridge runners, and participant lists on window creation.

nodes.name: ReInitializeParticipants
nodes.summary: Re-creates the participant list UI from the current participant data model, rebuilding Ollama and Cloud AI cards.

nodes.name: OpenProject
nodes.summary: Loads a project from disk, validates participant configurations, initializes project-specific services, and switches the UI to the active project view.

nodes.name: BuildProjectCard
nodes.summary: Dynamically constructs a rich WPF project card for the project gallery, including thumbnails, participant badges, and action buttons.

nodes.name: ShowProjectTypePicker
nodes.summary: Displays the project type selection dialog with visual cards for each available project type, used when creating new projects.

nodes.name: ShowMismatchBlockDialog
nodes.summary: Shows a blocking dialog when project participants don't match the current configuration, offering options to resolve mismatches before opening.

nodes.name: ShowProjectParticipantFixDialog
nodes.summary: Interactive dialog for resolving participant mismatches by mapping saved project participants to currently configured AI providers.

nodes.name: BuildFilesContent
nodes.summary: Constructs the project file manager panel, organizing project files into sections by folder with version history support.

nodes.name: BuildFileRow
nodes.summary: Creates an interactive row UI element for a single project file with expand/collapse versions, open-in-editor, and copy actions.

nodes.name: BuildRoadmapContent
nodes.summary: Constructs the entire roadmap panel UI with milestones, items, progress bars, and image zones from the current roadmap data model.

nodes.name: BuildItemRow
nodes.summary: Builds an individual roadmap item row with status indicator, assignee, deadline, progress bar, and inline editing controls.

nodes.name: ShowMilestoneDialog
nodes.summary: Presents a dialog for creating or editing a roadmap milestone with rich text description, status, and assignee fields.

nodes.name: ShowItemDialog
nodes.summary: Presents a dialog for creating or editing a roadmap task item with fields for title, assignee, deadline, status, and notes.

nodes.name: BuildRoadmapHtml
nodes.summary: Generates a styled HTML export of the project roadmap including all milestones and items with progress indicators.

nodes.name: ApplyRoadmapCommands
nodes.summary: Processes structured AI-generated roadmap update commands, applying add/update/delete operations to milestones and items.

nodes.name: BuildBridgeContent
nodes.summary: Constructs the bridge mode panel with MCP, controller, and agents sub-sections for the multi-agent automation workflow.

nodes.name: BuildBridgeMcpSection
nodes.summary: Builds the MCP server configuration section in the bridge panel including tool toggles, client setup instructions, and server status.

nodes.name: BuildBridgeControllerSection
nodes.summary: Builds the controller agent configuration section in bridge mode, managing the Claude agent that orchestrates other bridge agents.

nodes.name: BuildBridgeAgentsSection
nodes.summary: Builds the bridge agent roster panel listing configured external agents with their status, tools, and management actions.

nodes.name: BuildMcpTools
nodes.summary: Constructs and registers all MCP tool definitions (file ops, web fetch, project management tools) exposed to bridge agents via the MCP server.

nodes.name: ShowAddAgentDialog
nodes.summary: Dialog for configuring and adding a new bridge agent with provider, model, system prompt, tool permissions, and rate limit settings.

nodes.name: BuildBridgeInstructions
nodes.summary: Assembles the system prompt and tool instructions for bridge mode agents describing available MCP tools and usage rules.

nodes.name: ShowProjectSettingsDialog
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project settings dialog managing participants, AI roles, tone, response length, language, and project-specific configuration.

nodes.name: ShowCharacterEditorDialog
nodes.summary: Full character creation/editing dialog with fields for name, portrait, backstory, personality, voice, and faction affiliations.

nodes.name: BuildCloudAICard
nodes.summary: Builds the configuration card UI for a Cloud AI participant (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.) with model picker, API key, and role settings.

nodes.name: BuildOllamaCard
nodes.summary: Builds the configuration card UI for a local Ollama participant with model selection from discovered local models and role settings.

nodes.name: TriggerAiResponsesAsync
nodes.summary: Dispatches AI response generation to the appropriate orchestration mode (all-respond, coordinator-first, coordinator-summarizes, coordinator-only).

nodes.name: RunAllRespondModeAsync
nodes.summary: Runs all active AI participants in parallel, collecting responses and orchestrating follow-up dialogue rounds.

nodes.name: RunCoordinatorFirstModeAsync
nodes.summary: Runs the coordinator AI first, then dispatches responses to other participants based on coordinator tagging.

nodes.name: RunOllamaStreamAsync
nodes.summary: Streams a response from a local Ollama model, handling token-by-token streaming, error states, and history updates.

nodes.name: RunCloudAIStreamAsync
nodes.summary: Streams a response from a Cloud AI provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.), handling multi-provider differences, tool use, and error recovery.

nodes.name: TriggerSuperPowersInterviewAsync
nodes.summary: Runs an automated interview between the user's AI assistant and a SuperPowers AI to generate personalized capability prompts for participants.

nodes.name: TriggerWorkSessionAsync
nodes.summary: Triggers a structured AI work session using coordinator and planner roles to drive project progress based on the current roadmap.

nodes.name: ProcessAIFileOperationTags
nodes.summary: Parses and executes structured file operation tags embedded in AI responses, writing, appending, or deleting project files as directed.

nodes.name: MaybeCompressHistoryAsync
nodes.summary: Checks if the shared conversation history exceeds the compression threshold and summarizes old messages to reduce token usage.

nodes.name: AddStreamingBubble
nodes.summary: Creates and manages an animated streaming message bubble in the chat panel, updating it in real-time as tokens arrive.

nodes.name: ShowFoldersSetupDialog
nodes.summary: Settings dialog for configuring project folder paths, output directory, and file access permissions for the application.

nodes.name: ShowClaudetteChatWindow
nodes.summary: Opens a floating chat window with Claudette (the built-in AI assistant) for help, tips, or interactive guidance about the application.

nodes.name: ApplyTheme
nodes.summary: Applies a selected theme by loading its resource dictionary and updating the Windows title bar color via DWM API.

nodes.name: BuildRoleInstruction
nodes.summary: Constructs a detailed system prompt section describing a participant's role, responsibilities, and interaction rules within the team.

nodes.name: BuildAppContextInstruction
nodes.summary: Builds the system prompt section describing the current application context, project state, and participant team composition.

nodes.name: CreateProjectBackupAsync
nodes.summary: Creates a timestamped ZIP backup of the current project folder, providing progress feedback during the backup operation.

nodes.name: BuildCloudAIHistoryFor
nodes.summary: Builds the message history array for a Cloud AI API request, filtering and formatting shared chat history for the specific provider.

nodes.name: MainWindow.xaml.cs.stash
nodes.summary: Stashed backup of the MainWindow code-behind file (~18,249 lines), likely representing a previous version or work-in-progress snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: File extension .stash indicates a manual backup rather than a tracked source file; not compiled by the build system.

nodes.name: ParticipantsWindow.cs
nodes.summary: WPF window for displaying and managing all AI participants (Cloud AI and Ollama) in a card-based layout, with sorting, status display, and model info dialogs.

nodes.name: ParticipantsWindow
nodes.summary: Standalone window showing all configured AI participants with provider color coding, status badges, sort controls, and per-participant edit and model-info dialogs.

nodes.name: BuildCard
nodes.summary: Constructs a rich participant card UI element showing provider badge, model name, status indicator, role, and action buttons.

nodes.name: ShowEditDialog
nodes.summary: Comprehensive dialog for editing a participant's configuration including model, API key, system prompt override, rate limit, and role settings.

nodes.name: ShowModelInfo
nodes.summary: Displays a detailed model information dialog with context window, pricing, capabilities, and usage notes for the selected AI model.

nodes.name: RebuildCards
nodes.summary: Refreshes and re-renders all participant cards in sorted order, applying the active sort mode and active-at-top preference.

nodes.name: PortraitCropDialog.cs
nodes.summary: WPF dialog for interactively cropping a portrait image to a fixed aspect ratio, supporting zoom and pan via mouse input and producing a cropped bitmap result.

nodes.name: PortraitCropDialog
nodes.summary: Interactive image cropping dialog with canvas-based pan and zoom, producing a fixed-size portrait crop as a BitmapSource.

nodes.name: BuildUI
nodes.summary: Programmatically constructs the full cropping dialog UI including preview canvas, zoom slider, pan controls, and action buttons.

nodes.name: ComputeCrop
nodes.summary: Calculates the pixel coordinates of the crop rectangle within the source image based on current pan offset, zoom level, and target frame dimensions.

nodes.name: SaveBitmap
nodes.summary: Renders the cropped image region to a RenderTargetBitmap at the target portrait frame size and stores it as the dialog result.

nodes.name: ProjectTypeEditorWindow.cs
nodes.summary: WPF window for creating and editing custom project types, supporting fields for name, icon, description, structure levels, world folders, feature flags, and seed file management.

nodes.name: ProjectTypeEditorWindow
nodes.summary: Editor window for defining custom project types with full CRUD operations, seed file management, XAML generation, and icon/symbol selection.

nodes.name: ProjectTypeEditorWindow
nodes.summary: Constructor that builds the entire editor window UI programmatically, including all form fields, action buttons, and seed files panel.

nodes.name: BuildXaml
nodes.summary: Generates the XAML markup string for a project type definition from the current form field values.

nodes.name: RebuildSeedFilesPanel
nodes.summary: Refreshes the seed files panel listing all template files associated with the current project type, with add and remove actions.

nodes.name: SaveType_Click
nodes.summary: Validates the form, generates XAML for the project type, and saves it as a file to the project types directory.

nodes.name: ShowAddSeedFileDialog
nodes.summary: Dialog for selecting or creating a new seed file template, choosing its target folder within the project structure.

nodes.name: README.txt
nodes.summary: Instructions for users explaining the purpose and usage of the INPUT folder for injecting files into AI context at the start of a session.

nodes.name: BusinessProject.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for business projects, specifying structure levels, features, and world folder configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom XAML schema defining a project type with properties consumed by the ProjectTypeEditorWindow and OpenProject logic.

nodes.name: GameProject.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for game development projects, enabling world building, roadmap, and game-specific structure levels.

nodes.name: General.xaml
nodes.summary: Default general-purpose project type XAML definition with minimal structure, serving as the fallback project type.

nodes.name: notes.md
nodes.summary: Seed notes file template for the General project type's PROJECTPLAN folder, providing a starting placeholder for project notes.

nodes.name: NonFictionBook.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for non-fiction book writing projects with chapter-based structure levels and relevant feature flags.

nodes.name: Novel.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for novel writing projects with world building enabled, character/location folders, and chapter structure.

nodes.name: RoleplayingCampaign.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for tabletop roleplaying campaign management, enabling world building with NPC and location folders.

nodes.name: SoftwareProject.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for software development projects with roadmap enabled and code-oriented structure levels.

nodes.name: Theatre.xaml
nodes.summary: Project type definition XAML for theatre production projects with stage directions support, scene/act structure levels, and character world folders.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview documentation for ClaudetRelay with installation instructions, feature descriptions (multi-agent chat, world builder, roadmap, bridge mode, MCP server), and supported AI provider list.

nodes.name: ICloudAIService.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the ICloudAIService interface and CloudAIMessage record that all cloud AI provider services must implement, providing a uniform API for sending, streaming, availability-checking, and model enumeration.

nodes.name: AnthropicService.cs
nodes.summary: Implements ICloudAIService for the Anthropic Claude API, handling both full-response and SSE streaming using the /v1/messages endpoint with Anthropic's specific message format and system-prompt convention.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses IAsyncEnumerable for streaming and Utf8JsonWriter for zero-allocation request serialization.

nodes.name: OpenAICompatibleService.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base class implementing the OpenAI /v1/chat/completions protocol, providing shared HTTP client setup, streaming, and JSON serialization for all OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Mistral, OpenRouter, xAI Grok, Ollama Cloud).
nodes.languageNotes: Concrete providers inherit and only supply a base URL and default model list, keeping all protocol logic in one place.

nodes.name: OpenAIService.cs
nodes.summary: Thin subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting the official OpenAI API endpoint with GPT-4o, GPT-4 Turbo, and GPT-3.5 Turbo as default models.

nodes.name: GoogleAIService.cs
nodes.summary: Implements ICloudAIService for Google's Generative Language API (Gemini), translating ClaudetRelay's message format into Google's contents/parts schema and stripping thinking tokens from Gemini Flash Thinking responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Converts 'assistant' role to 'model' per Google API spec, and filters out <thinking> parts to prevent internal reasoning from leaking to the user.

nodes.name: GroqService.cs
nodes.summary: Thin subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting the Groq inference API with Llama 3.3, Mixtral, and Gemma2 as default models.

nodes.name: MistralService.cs
nodes.summary: Thin subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting the Mistral AI API with small, medium, and large Mistral models as defaults.

nodes.name: OpenRouterService.cs
nodes.summary: Subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting OpenRouter, with a curated model-list override that filters the 600+ entry catalogue down to models from recognised providers and strips variant suffixes.

nodes.name: XAIGrokService.cs
nodes.summary: Thin subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting the xAI Grok API endpoint with Grok-3, Grok-3-fast, Grok-3-mini, and Grok-2 as default models.

nodes.name: OllamaOpenAIService.cs
nodes.summary: Subclass of OpenAICompatibleService targeting Ollama's cloud API endpoint, overriding availability checking to accept HTTP 403 (which Ollama Cloud returns for invalid keys) in addition to 200 and 401.

nodes.name: OllamaService.cs
nodes.summary: Self-contained HTTP client for locally-running Ollama instances, supporting full-response and streaming via the Ollama /api/chat endpoint, thinking-token extraction for qwen3/reasoning models, and model metadata retrieval via /api/show.
nodes.languageNotes: Does not implement ICloudAIService directly — uses its own OllamaChatMessage record; exposes a ThinkingUpdated event for UI-side reasoning visualization.

nodes.name: McpServer.cs
nodes.summary: Lightweight in-process HTTP + Server-Sent Events server implementing the Model Context Protocol (spec 2024-11-05), exposing registered AI participants as MCP tools callable by Claude Desktop and other MCP clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses HttpListener and ConcurrentDictionary for session management; SSE keepalive pings every 15 s prevent proxy timeouts.

nodes.name: ModelControllerRunner.cs
nodes.summary: Implements the multi-round agentic loop that lets a controller model (Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, or Ollama ReAct) orchestrate tool calls against registered McpTools, maintaining separate persistent conversation histories per API format.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports three agentic loop strategies: native Anthropic tool-use API, OpenAI-format tool-calls, and a ReAct XML prompt-based fallback for Ollama and unsupported providers.

nodes.name: ProviderRateLimiter.cs
nodes.summary: Thread-safe rate limiter that serialises cloud API calls through a SemaphoreSlim, enforcing a configurable requests-per-minute budget by computing and awaiting the minimum inter-request interval.

nodes.name: ExportService.cs
nodes.summary: Static service that generates self-contained HTML and Markdown export documents from a project's chat log, assigning stable per-participant colour palettes for AI bubbles in the HTML output.

nodes.name: GeneralChatLogService.cs
nodes.summary: Static service managing the rolling two-segment JSON chat log for general (non-project) chat, handling segment rotation at 500 entries and an accumulating AI-generated summary.md file.

nodes.name: SettingsService.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the AppSettings model (participants, bridge/MCP configuration, UI preferences, rate-limit settings) and the SettingsService that persists it as JSON, including one-time data migrations for legacy config fields.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains several co-located data models: BridgeFolder, BridgeAgent, ProviderThrottleSettings, ParticipantConfig — all serialized within AppSettings.

nodes.name: ProjectService.cs
nodes.summary: Central project management service defining the complete project data model (ProjectSettings, OrchestrationMode, roles, ChatLogEntry) and all file-system operations for creating, loading, saving, sandboxing, and managing project chatlog segments.
nodes.languageNotes: IsPathSafe and SafeWriteFile implement sandboxing to prevent AI-generated paths from escaping the project folder.

nodes.name: ProjectTypeDefinition.cs
nodes.summary: Data class describing a project type loaded from XAML ResourceDictionaries, carrying identity, structural hierarchy, world-building folder names, and feature flags that control which UI panels are available for that project type.

nodes.name: RoadmapService.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the roadmap data model (Roadmap, RoadmapMilestone, RoadmapItem) and the RoadmapService that persists it as XML, plus GetContextText which formats the roadmap into AI system-prompt text including command syntax for AI-driven updates.

nodes.name: ThumbnailService.cs
nodes.summary: Static WPF utility service that generates and caches 200-px thumbnails alongside originals in a _thumbs subfolder, loading them memory-efficiently via BitmapImage.DecodePixelWidth.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses WPF BitmapImage with CacheOption.OnLoad and Freeze() to ensure thread-safe, GC-independent bitmap lifetime.

nodes.name: WindowsCredentialManager.cs
nodes.summary: Thin P/Invoke wrapper around the Windows Credential Manager (advapi32.dll) that stores and retrieves provider API keys under target names of the form 'ClaudetRelay:{provider}'.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses GCHandle.Alloc(Pinned) to pass byte arrays to the unmanaged CREDENTIAL struct; pins are freed in a finally block.

nodes.name: SelfDescriptionService.cs
nodes.summary: Fire-and-forget service that queries each configured AI model with structured prompts to obtain a role title, one-sentence description, and likes/dislikes, persisting the results to AppSettings for display on participant cards.

nodes.name: OxsuitLoader.cs
nodes.summary: Parses native OXSUIT 1.0 theme files (.oxsuit XML) into WPF ResourceDictionaries, mapping OXSUIT short colour-key names to WPF brush keys and converting the RRGGBBAA colour format to WPF's AARRGGBB convention.
nodes.languageNotes: ApplyFallbacks ensures OXSUIT themes that omit optional keys still render correctly by copying from semantically equivalent brushes.

nodes.name: WorldEntityService.cs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive world-building data layer defining WorldEntity, EntityBoardData, BoardRelation, and related types, with WorldEntityService, EntityBoardService, and WorldBoardRegistryService handling CRUD and board-layout persistence in the project's PROJECTPLAN folder.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains stale-file cleanup logic and a silent plural-to-singular folder migration to maintain backward compatibility with older project structures.

nodes.name: ICloudAIService
nodes.summary: Shared contract for all cloud AI provider services, defining async send, async stream, availability check, and model-list methods.

nodes.name: AnthropicService
nodes.summary: Implements ICloudAIService against the Anthropic Claude API, handling message construction, streaming SSE parsing, and model enumeration.

nodes.name: OpenAICompatibleService
nodes.summary: Abstract base for all OpenAI-protocol providers; supplies SendAsync, StreamAsync, and shared HTTP/JSON scaffolding.

nodes.name: McpServer
nodes.summary: HTTP+SSE MCP server managing session lifecycle, JSON-RPC dispatch, tool registration, and keepalive pumping.

nodes.name: McpTool
nodes.summary: Descriptor for one AI participant exposed as an MCP tool, carrying name, description, input schema override, and execution callbacks.

nodes.name: ModelControllerRunner
nodes.summary: Multi-round agentic loop runner supporting Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, and ReAct strategies with persistent per-format conversation history.

nodes.name: ProviderRateLimiter
nodes.summary: Thread-safe request rate limiter using SemaphoreSlim and configurable RPM budget.

nodes.name: AppSettings
nodes.summary: Root settings model holding participant configs, bridge/MCP options, UI preferences, rate-limit budgets, and file-size limits.

nodes.name: ProjectSettings
nodes.summary: Complete project state: identity, orchestration mode, participant roles, roadmap flags, and optional bridge agent roster.

nodes.name: RoadmapService
nodes.summary: Persists and loads roadmaps as XML and generates AI-ingestible context text including command syntax for roadmap updates.

nodes.name: WorldEntityService
nodes.summary: CRUD service for WorldEntity objects persisted as JSON files in PROJECTPLAN/{EntityType}/, with portrait/image file management and legacy folder migration.

nodes.name: EntityBoardService
nodes.summary: Loads and saves EntityBoardData (positions, relations, frames) per board ID, with migration from the legacy _board__world.json filename.

nodes.name: OllamaService
nodes.summary: HTTP client for local Ollama instances with streaming, thinking-token extraction, model info retrieval, and multi-format content extraction fallback.

nodes.name: StreamAsync
nodes.summary: Streams tokens from the Anthropic SSE response, parsing content_block_delta events and yielding text tokens incrementally.

nodes.name: HandleToolsCallAsync
nodes.summary: Dispatches an MCP tools/call JSON-RPC request to the matching McpTool's ExecuteAsync or QueryAsync callback and formats the result as an MCP content block.

nodes.name: RunAsync
nodes.summary: Entry point for one user turn in the agentic loop, routing to the provider-specific implementation based on the configured controller provider.

nodes.name: RunAnthropicAsync
nodes.summary: Anthropic-native agentic loop: appends messages to persistent history, iterates up to 20 rounds of tool-use, and collects the final text response.

nodes.name: RunOpenAiCompatAsync
nodes.summary: OpenAI-compatible agentic loop supporting Groq, OpenRouter, and Mistral, maintaining persistent history with system seeding on the first turn.

nodes.name: RunReActAsync
nodes.summary: ReAct-pattern fallback for Ollama and unsupported providers: prompts the model with XML tool-call syntax, parses <tool_call> tags, executes tools, and feeds results back as <tool_result> blocks.

nodes.name: CreateProject
nodes.summary: Creates a new project folder with standard subfolders (INPUT, OUTPUT, PROJECTPLAN, AI-Characters, PROJECTSETTINGS) and type-specific world-building subdirectories.

nodes.name: IsPathSafe
nodes.summary: Sandboxing guard that resolves both paths to full canonical form and verifies the target resides inside the project folder to prevent path traversal by AI-generated outputs.

nodes.name: GetContextText
nodes.summary: Formats the full roadmap as structured plain-text for injection into AI system prompts, including update/complete command syntax and coordinator-only commands for adding milestones and items.

nodes.name: FetchAndSaveAsync
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the two-phase model self-description flow: temperature-0 description/title fetch followed by default-temperature likes/dislikes fetch, persisting results to AppSettings.

nodes.name: GenerateHtml
nodes.summary: Generates a self-contained HTML export of a chat log with per-participant colour-coded bubbles, configurable font/bubble-width, and embedded CSS.

nodes.name: StreamAsync
nodes.summary: Streams tokens from a local Ollama /api/chat response, extracting thinking tokens from qwen3 models and firing the ThinkingUpdated event, then yielding content tokens.

nodes.name: SettingsWindow.xaml
nodes.summary: XAML layout and style definitions for the Settings window, declaring custom control templates for ComboBox, TabControl, Button, TextBox, and PasswordBox that bind to dynamic OXSUIT theme brush resources.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses DynamicResource bindings throughout so the window instantly reflects whichever OXSUIT theme is loaded at runtime. All tab content is added programmatically from the code-behind.

nodes.name: SettingsWindow.xaml.cs
nodes.summary: Code-behind for SettingsWindow that dynamically builds the General, Providers, and per-participant UI tabs at runtime, manages provider API key forms, validates participant nicknames for uniqueness, tests Ollama/Cloud AI connections, and persists all settings on save.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sealed nested types (ParticipantForm, ProviderKeyForm) to bundle per-tab control references. Provider tabs are constructed imperatively in C# rather than declaratively in XAML for maximum flexibility. Async test methods use fire-and-forget lambda patterns (async (_, _) =>) wired to button Click events.

nodes.name: AdeptusCustodes.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Adeptus Custodes Warhammer 40K faction, featuring a dark near-black background with rich gold and amber palette applied to all UI surfaces.

nodes.name: AdeptusMechanicus.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Adeptus Mechanicus Warhammer 40K faction, defining color and design tokens for a dark industrial aesthetic with mechanicus red and steel tones.

nodes.name: Aegis.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme named Aegis, defining a full palette of color and design tokens for a protective shield aesthetic applied across all UI surface areas.

nodes.name: AethelGardens.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme named Aethel Gardens, applying a nature-inspired color palette with garden and botanical tones across all UI surfaces.

nodes.name: AmberGlowTerminal.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme emulating classic amber-phosphor terminal monitors with a dark background and warm amber/orange accent colors for a retro computing aesthetic.

nodes.name: AnvilForge.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme named Anvil Forge, applying a dark industrial blacksmith aesthetic with forge-fire accent colors across all UI surfaces.

nodes.name: AopoaXian.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Star Citizen Aopoa Xi'an faction, defining a full OXSUIT color and token set for a sleek alien aesthetic.

nodes.name: ArcticAurora.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by arctic aurora borealis, applying icy blue and purple-green aurora accent colors on a dark near-black background.

nodes.name: ArgoAstronautics.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by Argo Astronautics (Star Citizen ship manufacturer), applying a space-exploration color palette with clean utility tones.

nodes.name: AstroBlueprint.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme styled after an engineering blueprint, using blueprint-blue backgrounds and white-line accent colors for a technical drawing aesthetic.

nodes.name: AtomicDawn.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme with a Fallout/atomic-age dawn aesthetic, blending warm orange-yellow sunrise tones on a dark retro background.

nodes.name: AutumnLeaves.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by autumn foliage, using warm orange, red, and gold tones drawn from seasonal leaf colors across all UI surfaces.

nodes.name: BloodAngels.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Blood Angels Warhammer 40K Space Marine chapter, using deep red and gold tones on a near-black background.

nodes.name: BrotherhoodOfSteel.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Brotherhood of Steel from the Fallout franchise, applying military steel-grey and olive tones with gold insignia accents.

nodes.name: CaesarsLegion.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by Caesar's Legion from Fallout: New Vegas, using Roman crimson and bronze tones with a dark austere background.

nodes.name: CaptainFuture.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the classic Captain Future sci-fi franchise, applying a retro space-opera color palette with vivid accent tones.

nodes.name: CarraraMarble.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme styled after Carrara white marble, applying light grey-white surface tones with cool veining accents for an elegant minimal aesthetic.

nodes.name: CatppuccinFrappe.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme implementing the Catppuccin Frappe palette — a muted medium-dark theme with cool blue-grey backgrounds and pastel accent colors.

nodes.name: CatppuccinLatte.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme implementing the Catppuccin Latte palette — the light variant of the Catppuccin family with warm cream backgrounds and pastel accents.

nodes.name: CatppuccinMacchiato.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme implementing the Catppuccin Macchiato palette — a rich dark variant with deep blue backgrounds and vibrant pastel accents.

nodes.name: CatppuccinMocha.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme implementing the Catppuccin Mocha palette — the darkest and most popular Catppuccin variant with deep navy-purple backgrounds and rose/lavender accents.

nodes.name: ChildrenOfAtom.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by the Children of Atom cult from the Fallout series, featuring glowing green radioactive accent tones on a dark irradiated background.

nodes.name: ChuckNorris.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme humorously named after Chuck Norris, applying bold action-hero color tones with strong contrasts across all UI surfaces.

nodes.name: SettingsWindow
nodes.summary: WPF Window code-behind that orchestrates the entire settings UI — builds tabbed interfaces for general preferences, per-provider API keys, and per-participant configuration including Ollama and Cloud AI provider sections.

nodes.name: BuildGeneralTab
nodes.summary: Imperatively builds the General tab UI with controls for user name, response tone slider, Mockingbird mode toggle, AI dialogue turns, response length, chattiness, and UI zoom — all wired to bidirectional value displays.

nodes.name: BuildProvidersTab
nodes.summary: Creates one settings tab per supported AI provider (Anthropic, Google AI, Groq, Mistral, Ollama cloud, OpenAI, OpenRouter, xAI) by iterating a fixed provider list.

nodes.name: BuildSingleProviderTab
nodes.summary: Constructs a single provider tab with a password/plain-text toggle API key input, show/hide button, hint text, and an async test connection button that verifies the stored key.

nodes.name: BuildTab
nodes.summary: Builds a full participant configuration tab containing enable toggle, nickname input with duplicate validation, provider type selector, and conditionally visible Ollama or Cloud AI sections including model picker with live search filter and RPM rate-limit controls.

nodes.name: ValidateAllNicknames
nodes.summary: Re-validates every participant tab's nickname against the user's own name and all other participants, showing or hiding the orange-red duplicate warning on each affected tab.

nodes.name: ApplyCloudModelFilter
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the cloud model ComboBox items from the master model list filtered by the current search text, preserving the previously selected model as a custom entry when it falls outside the filtered view.

nodes.name: TestOllamaAsync
nodes.summary: Asynchronously tests the Ollama server connection for a participant form, and on success populates the model combo box with the live list of available models from the server.

nodes.name: TestCloudAIAsync
nodes.summary: Asynchronously tests a cloud AI provider connection using the stored API key, and on success fetches and displays the live model list with a count in the status label.

nodes.name: SaveAll_Click
nodes.summary: Click handler that persists all settings on save — in provider mode saves API keys to Windows Credential Manager; in general mode writes user name, tone, dialogue settings, zoom, all participant configs, and per-provider RPM throttle settings.

nodes.name: ApplyTitleBarTheme
nodes.summary: Applies the active OXSUIT theme's sidebar colors to the Windows DWM title bar using P/Invoke — setting dark mode flag, caption background color, and caption text color for Win10/Win11.

nodes.name: ClaudesChoice.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Visual theme named 'Claudes Choice' featuring a deep navy/dark-teal color scheme with teal primary accent (#18C0B4), amber secondary accent, and violet tertiary accent; designed as an opinionated default aesthetic for the ClaudetRelay app.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard oxsuit v1.0 XML schema defining 30 named color tokens across 8 surface slots plus 9 layout/border tokens.

nodes.name: ClearDay.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Light-mode visual theme named 'Clear Day' using a clean white/pale-blue palette with a vibrant blue primary accent (#3078D8); suitable for bright ambient environments.

nodes.name: ConsolidatedOutland.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark post-apocalyptic visual theme named 'Consolidated Outland' inspired by the Star Citizen universe, using near-black backgrounds with warm parchment text and an orange amber accent (#E89030).

nodes.name: CosmicNoir.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark space-noir visual theme named 'Cosmic Noir' using deep midnight-blue backgrounds with lavender text and a hot-pink accent (#F472B6), complemented by violet and teal secondary accents.

nodes.name: Crusader.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark medieval-military visual theme named 'Crusader' using deep navy backgrounds with light steel-blue text and a gold accent (#E0A028), evoking crusader heraldry with blue secondary tones.

nodes.name: Dracula.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Visual theme named 'Dracula' implementing the popular Dracula color scheme with charcoal backgrounds (#282A36), cream text (#F8F8F2), and a pink primary accent (#FF79C6) plus purple, green, and cyan secondary accents.

nodes.name: DragonBallZ.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark anime-inspired visual theme named 'Dragon Ball Z' using near-black backgrounds with warm cream text and a bright golden-yellow Super Saiyan accent (#FFD820), complemented by blue and orange secondaries.

nodes.name: DrakeSalvage.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark industrial visual theme named 'Drake Salvage' inspired by the Star Citizen Drake Interplanetary aesthetic, using near-black backgrounds with aged parchment text and a deep orange accent (#F08030).

nodes.name: DwarfForgeMastery.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark forge-fantasy visual theme named 'Dwarf Forge Mastery' evoking Dwarf Fortress aesthetics, using deep brown-black backgrounds with warm stone-grey text and a blazing red-orange accent (#FF4400).

nodes.name: Earth.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark atmospheric visual theme named 'Earth' evoking a view from orbit, using deep space-blue backgrounds with soft sky-blue text and a bright ice-white highlight accent (#D8EEFF), with blue and green secondaries.

nodes.name: Eldar.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Eldar-inspired visual theme using a distinctive dual-surface design: warm parchment content areas (#F0EAD8) contrasted with deep teal sidebar/controls, unified by an arcane purple accent (#A060D8).
nodes.languageNotes: Unique among oxsuit themes in this batch for mixing a warm light content surface with dark sidebar surfaces — most other themes use a consistent dark or light palette throughout.

nodes.name: EmergencyBreachTerminal.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Monochromatic alarm-red visual theme named 'Emergency Breach Terminal' using near-black backgrounds with red text (#FF6666) and a crimson accent (#CC0000), evoking a security-breach terminal display.

nodes.name: Esperia.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark exotic visual theme named 'Esperia' inspired by the Star Citizen Esperia brand, using near-black backgrounds with warm cream text and a golden-amber accent (#E0A818), evoking alien-artifact craftsmanship.

nodes.name: ForestBloom.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark nature visual theme named 'Forest Bloom' using deep forest-green backgrounds with soft sage-green text and a burnt-orange accent (#D35400), evoking an autumnal woodland atmosphere.

nodes.name: gatac.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark sci-fi visual theme named 'gatac' using near-black deep-violet backgrounds with pale lavender text and a vivid electric-purple accent (#C060FF), evoking advanced alien or stealth-craft aesthetics.

nodes.name: GenestealerCult.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Genestealer Cult-inspired visual theme using dark purple backgrounds with lavender text and a hot-magenta accent (#E010C0), evoking psychic infestation and cult iconography.

nodes.name: GreycatIndustrial.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark industrial visual theme named 'Greycat Industrial' inspired by the Star Citizen Greycat brand, using near-black backgrounds with khaki-olive text and a bright yellow accent (#E0D030), evoking heavy industrial machinery.

nodes.name: GruvboxDark.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Visual theme named 'Gruvbox Dark' implementing the popular retro-groove Gruvbox color scheme with warm charcoal backgrounds (#282828), cream text (#EBDBB2), and a yellow primary accent (#FABD2F) plus mauve, green, and aqua secondaries.

nodes.name: HelloKitty.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Light playful visual theme named 'Hello Kitty' using white and pink pastel backgrounds with crimson text and a vivid pink accent (#FF0056), evoking the iconic Sanrio Hello Kitty brand aesthetic.

nodes.name: HighContrastBright.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Accessibility-oriented light visual theme named 'High Contrast Bright' using pure white backgrounds with pure black text and a bold blue accent (#0000EE), maximizing legibility for users with visual impairments.

nodes.name: HighContrastDark.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Accessibility-oriented dark visual theme named 'High Contrast Dark' using pure black backgrounds with pure white text and a saturated yellow accent (#FFFF00), maximizing legibility in low-light environments.

nodes.name: InuYasha.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark anime-inspired visual theme named 'Inu Yasha' using deep indigo-black backgrounds with warm parchment text and a bold crimson-red accent (#E03828), referencing the InuYasha series palette of red and purple.

nodes.name: IrradiatedWastelandCore.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark post-apocalyptic visual theme named 'Irradiated Wasteland Core' evoking a Fallout wasteland aesthetic, using brown-tinged dark backgrounds with ashen text and a radioactive-red accent (#D42020).

nodes.name: Jupiter.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark planetary visual theme named 'Jupiter' using warm brown-ochre backgrounds with cream text and a deep crimson accent (#C82010), evoking the Great Red Spot and banded cloud layers of Jupiter.

nodes.name: LeatherboundBlack.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dual-surface visual theme named 'Leatherbound Black' contrasting warm parchment content areas (#F4E8CC) with dark charcoal sidebar/controls, unified by aged-gold accents (#A08858 / #D4AA3A), evoking an antique leather-bound tome.

nodes.name: LeatherboundBlue.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Leatherbound Blue' visual theme with warm parchment content surfaces paired with a deep navy sidebar and gold accent highlights, evoking an aged leather-bound book aesthetic.
nodes.languageNotes: OXSUIT XML theme format: 30 named color tokens across 9 surface categories (Content, Sidebar, Control, Input, Accent, Primary, Secondary, Tertiary) plus 9 design tokens for radius and border widths.

nodes.name: LeatherboundBrown.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Leatherbound Brown' visual theme with warm parchment content surfaces, deep brown sidebar, and amber-orange highlights evoking rich brown leather bookbinding.

nodes.name: LeatherboundGreen.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Leatherbound Green' visual theme with parchment-toned content areas, dark forest-green sidebar, and gold/sage green accents representing green leather bookbinding.

nodes.name: LeatherboundRed.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Leatherbound Red' visual theme with warm parchment content, crimson sidebar tones, and bold red highlights completing the Leatherbound series with a red leather variant.

nodes.name: MagicMushroomMayhem.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Magic Mushroom Mayhem' visual theme using near-black backgrounds with vivid purple accents, electric yellow highlights, and neon pink/green/cyan tertiary accents for a psychedelic palette.

nodes.name: Mars.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Mars' planetary theme with deep dark-red backgrounds, warm sandy text tones, and a fiery orange-red accent inspired by the Martian surface landscape.

nodes.name: MeadowBloom.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Meadow Bloom' visual theme using soft sage-green and off-white light surfaces with warm golden-yellow highlights and pastel green/teal secondary accents for a fresh outdoor feel.

nodes.name: Mercury.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Mercury' planetary theme with cool dark charcoal surfaces, slate-blue text, and warm bronze-gold accents representing the rocky, metallic character of the innermost planet.

nodes.name: MidnightCoffee.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Midnight Coffee' visual theme with very dark espresso-brown backgrounds, warm cream text, and soft peach-orange highlight tones evoking a late-night coffee atmosphere.

nodes.name: MinimalistContrast.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Minimalist Contrast' visual theme using near-black backgrounds with near-white text and a bold orange accent for a high-contrast minimalist design aesthetic.

nodes.name: MiraiConduit.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Mirai Conduit' visual theme with near-black deep-blue backgrounds, light icy-cyan text, and electric teal accents evoking a futuristic cyberpunk data-conduit aesthetic.

nodes.name: MISC.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'MISC' visual theme (inspired by the Star Citizen spacecraft brand) with dark near-black backgrounds, warm beige text, amber-orange accents, and steel-blue tertiary highlights.

nodes.name: Naruto.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Naruto' visual theme with deep dark backgrounds, warm parchment text, red-orange primary and scroll-blue secondary accents reflecting the ninja anime series palette.

nodes.name: NCR.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'NCR' visual theme (inspired by Fallout's New California Republic) with near-black desert-brown backgrounds, sandy khaki text, NCR-red highlights, and warm gold-amber accents.

nodes.name: Necrons.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Necrons' visual theme (inspired by Warhammer 40K Necrons) using very dark near-black backgrounds with cold green text and vivid undead-green highlights.

nodes.name: Neo-NoirRainyDistrict.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Neo-Noir Rainy District' visual theme with very dark purple-black backgrounds, cool blue-white text, amber street-light highlights, and neon cyan/pink secondary accents for a cyberpunk noir atmosphere.

nodes.name: NeonWarp.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Neon Warp' visual theme using near-black deep-navy backgrounds with icy-cyan text, yellow highlight, and full-saturation magenta/cyan/hot-pink accents for maximum neon intensity.

nodes.name: Neptune.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Neptune' planetary theme with deep midnight-blue backgrounds, light periwinkle text, and vibrant electric-blue accents inspired by the distant ice giant planet.

nodes.name: Newspaper.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Newspaper' visual theme using pure white and light-grey surfaces with stark near-black text and greyscale-only accent values, mimicking classic printed newsprint.

nodes.name: NordsLoreScroll.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Nords Lore Scroll' visual theme with dark warm-brown surfaces, aged parchment-cream text, deep crimson highlights, and muted purple/green/tan secondary accents evoking a Nordic fantasy manuscript.

nodes.name: ObsidianDeep.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Obsidian Deep' visual theme with near-black purple-tinted backgrounds, cool blue-grey text, vivid red primary accent, and electric purple/mint tertiary accents for a dark gemstone aesthetic.

nodes.name: OriginJumpworks.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Origin Jumpworks' visual theme (inspired by the Star Citizen luxury spacecraft brand) with near-black backgrounds, warm ivory text, rich gold accents, and periwinkle tertiary highlights.

nodes.name: PaperWhite.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Paper White' visual theme with warm off-white and cream light surfaces, dark warm-grey text, and a muted blue primary accent for a clean, readable e-reader-style appearance.

nodes.name: PinkFluffyUnicorns.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Pink Fluffy Unicorns' visual theme with white-pink and hot-pink surfaces, deep magenta text, golden-yellow highlights, and pastel pink/violet/sky-blue accents for a playful fantasy aesthetic.

nodes.name: Pluto.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Defines the 'Pluto' planetary theme with very dark reddish-brown backgrounds, warm sandy-cream text, and soft amber-tan highlights inspired by the distant dwarf planet's icy, rocky terrain.

nodes.name: RainbowSparkleFantasy.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Light pastel fantasy theme using lavender backgrounds, pink and aqua accents, and soft gold highlights — evokes a whimsical, rainbow-sparkle aesthetic.

nodes.name: RedIsFastah.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Ork-inspired dark red theme with near-black backgrounds, deep crimson sidebar, Rosso Corsa accent (#CC0000), and looted gold text — styled to comic in-universe commentary.
nodes.languageNotes: Inline XML comment in Ork dialect ("Da OrcBoyz painted dis one") makes this a novelty/humour theme.

nodes.name: RetroSynthwave.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Dark synthwave-inspired theme with deep indigo/purple backgrounds, magenta and cyan accents, and gold highlights — captures 1980s retro-futuristic neon aesthetics.

nodes.name: RetroTerminalGlow.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Classic phosphor-green CRT terminal theme with near-black backgrounds and bright #00FF00 text — emulates vintage computer terminal aesthetics.

nodes.name: RockAndStone.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Deep Space Rock (Valheim/Deep Rock Galactic-inspired) dark earthy theme with near-black brown backgrounds, warm gold-tan text, and amber orange accent — industrial mining aesthetic.

nodes.name: RSIProtocol.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Star Citizen (Roberts Space Industries) inspired dark navy-blue theme with cool steel-blue tones, amber highlight, and layered blue sidebar borders.

nodes.name: Salamanders.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Salamanders Space Marine chapter theme — dark forest-green backgrounds with vivid orange-red (#E84A08) fire accent on control borders, evoking the chapter's volcanic flame iconography.

nodes.name: Saturn.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Planetary Saturn theme with warm cream and tan light backgrounds, golden-ochre accents, and amber highlights — evokes Saturn's banded atmospheric palette.

nodes.name: SavannahAtDusk.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warm savannah-at-dusk light theme using sandy beige backgrounds, dusty sage-green dim tones, and burnt sienna accent — evokes an African savannah twilight palette.

nodes.name: Skyrim.oxsuit
nodes.summary: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim inspired dark slate-blue theme with cool steel-blue text and icy blue highlights — evokes the frost-bitten Nordic mountain palette of the game.

nodes.name: SlimePopMalfunction.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Chaotic psychedelic dark theme with deep purple-black backgrounds, neon magenta, acid-green, and cyan accents against yellow highlights — deliberately clashing neon-pop aesthetic.

nodes.name: StartWearingPurple.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Rich all-purple dark theme with deep indigo backgrounds, lavender text, and vivid violet-magenta accent (#CC50FF) — monochromatic purple palette evocative of the Gogol Bordello song.

nodes.name: SynapticMatrix.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Neural-network/AI dark theme using near-black backgrounds, cool periwinkle-blue text, cyan and teal accents, and vivid coral-red highlights — evokes an AI/matrix neural interface.

nodes.name: Tau.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K T'au Empire theme — warm light beige backgrounds with dark text and vivid orange (#E87000) accent paired with blue secondary, reflecting the faction's Sept color scheme.

nodes.name: TheEnclave.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Fallout: The Enclave faction theme — near-black military dark backgrounds with muted olive-green text and vivid emerald (#20B858) accent, reflecting the Enclave's advanced military identity.

nodes.name: TheInstitute.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Fallout 4: The Institute faction theme — cold dark navy backgrounds with pale blue text and electric cyan (#00E8FF) accent, evoking the secretive synth-making organization's sterile aesthetic.

nodes.name: TheMinutemen.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Fallout 4: The Minutemen faction theme — dark navy backgrounds with cool blue-grey text and colonial gold (#D4A828) accent, evoking the faction's patriot-militia heritage.

nodes.name: TheRailroad.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Fallout 4: The Railroad faction theme — near-black dark-brown backgrounds with warm parchment text and deep crimson-red (#E03428) accent, reflecting the underground resistance movement's gritty aesthetic.

nodes.name: ThousandSons.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Thousand Sons Chaos Space Marine theme — dark royal-blue backgrounds with warm ivory text, electric teal highlights, and gold (#E8A800) accent, reflecting the sorcerous Egyptian-inspired faction.

nodes.name: TokyoNight.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Tokyo Night dark theme with deep midnight-blue backgrounds, lavender-blue text, coral-pink accent (#F7768E), and purple/green/cyan secondaries — based on the popular Tokyo Night editor color scheme.

nodes.name: TotalLimeDestructionToad.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Vibrant chaotic dark theme with deep purple backgrounds, acid-yellow (#FFFF00) highlights, lime-green and hot-pink accents — intentionally absurdist, maximalist neon palette.

nodes.name: TumbrilLand.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Star Citizen Tumbril Land vehicle / military green theme — very dark olive-green backgrounds with khaki text and yellow-green (#90A838) accent, evoking armored ground vehicle aesthetics.

nodes.name: Ultramarines.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Warhammer 40K Ultramarines Space Marine chapter theme — deep royal-blue backgrounds with pale blue text and bright gold (#E8C800) accent, directly reflecting the chapter's iconic blue-and-gold heraldry.

nodes.name: Uranus.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Planetary Uranus theme — dark teal-navy backgrounds with pale cyan-aqua text and medium teal (#28A0B0) accent, capturing the planet's distinctive blue-green atmospheric color.

nodes.name: VaultTec.oxsuit
nodes.summary: Fallout: Vault-Tec corporation theme — dark navy backgrounds with warm ivory text and bold amber (#F0AD00) accent paired with steel-blue secondary, evoking the corporation's retro-futuristic yellow-and-blue branding.

nodes.name: Venus.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme defining a warm cream-and-amber palette with burnt-orange accents, giving the UI a parchment-like daytime feel. Covers all nine surface slots (Content, Sidebar, Control, Input, Primary, Secondary, Tertiary) plus Accent and design tokens.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the OXSUIT XML schema: a flat list of named color keys plus design tokens (CornerRadius, ShadowDepth, border widths). The transparent ContentBorder (#00000000) is a deliberate borderless style choice.

nodes.name: VioletViolation.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme with a high-contrast violet/purple dark sidebar contrasted against a near-white content area, hot-pink primary accent, and a jarring lime-green secondary accent for an intentionally bold aesthetic.
nodes.languageNotes: SecondaryAccent (#508000 — a muted green) contrasts strongly with the hot-pink primary, creating an intentional chromatic dissonance aligned with the 'Violation' theme name.

nodes.name: Waaaaagh.oxsuit
nodes.summary: OXSUIT theme inspired by Warhammer 40K Ork aesthetics: very dark near-black backgrounds with muted military-green text and a vivid lime (#78CC00) accent, evoking crude Ork technology and night-vision displays.
nodes.languageNotes: ContentBg (#121A0E) is a deep near-black green; the single vivid AccentBg (#78CC00) is the classic Ork-green war colour. All surface slots use closely related dark greens, creating a monochromatic yet intense look.

nodes.name: UiZoomHelper.cs
nodes.summary: Static utility class that applies a uniform scale transform to any WPF Window via LayoutTransform, reads the current zoom level from AppSettings, and formats zoom factors as percentage labels. Used by all window classes that need to honour the app-wide UI zoom setting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LayoutTransform (not RenderTransform) so WPF's measure/arrange pass sees scaled dimensions — this allows SizeToContent dialogs to auto-size correctly and avoids clipping.

nodes.name: Apply
nodes.summary: Applies a clamped zoom factor as a LayoutTransform ScaleTransform to the window's root content, and optionally scales the window's fixed Width/Height dimensions while skipping SizeToContent-managed axes.

nodes.name: FromSettings
nodes.summary: Loads the UiZoom value from persisted AppSettings via SettingsService and returns it clamped to the valid [0.25, 4.0] range.

nodes.name: FormatLabel
nodes.summary: Formats a zoom factor double as a percentage string (e.g. 1.5 → "150%") for display in zoom controls.

nodes.name: WorldBoardWindow.cs
nodes.summary: Standalone WPF Window providing the free-canvas World Builder board editor: renders draggable entity cards, named directional relations with waypoints, board pins, text boxes, frames, and a floating legend on a scrollable canvas. Supports rubber-band multi-selection, pan, snap-to-grid, zoom, filter/sort, auto-arrange, Ctrl+C/V copy-paste, and background images.
nodes.languageNotes: At ~4353 lines this is the largest file in the batch. The entire UI is built imperatively in C# (no XAML); BuildBoardContent() is the main render pass that tears down and reconstructs all canvas children on every state change.

nodes.name: BuildBoardContent
nodes.summary: Core render pass that constructs the entire board UI from scratch: toolbar with action buttons, collapsible filter panel, snap/zoom controls, canvas with background image, frames, relations, text boxes, entity cards, board pins, and the floating legend overlay.

nodes.name: GetFilteredAndSortedEntities
nodes.summary: Filters a list of world entities by name, faction, arc, alignment, and Lore-specific fields (character membership, common/historical knowledge tags), then sorts by name or creation date.

nodes.name: BuildAddContextMenu
nodes.summary: Builds the contextual 'Add' dropdown menu, offering entity-type pickers for each board entity type, a text box option, a background image option, a board-pin submenu, and a frame option, placing items at the specified canvas coordinates.

nodes.name: ShowGridSettingsDialog
nodes.summary: Shows an inline modal dialog for configuring the board grid: visibility toggle, opacity slider, colour swatch picker with theme-default reset, and grid size input. Persists changes via EntityBoardService.

nodes.name: ShowBackgroundImageDialog
nodes.summary: Shows a modal dialog for attaching a background image to the board canvas, with mode selection (Fill/Uniform/UniformToFill/Repeat/Scale) and scale percentage input.

nodes.name: BuildBoardPin
nodes.summary: Builds the visual tile for a pinned board reference: a resizable bordered card showing the board's symbol and name with a drag-resize grip in the bottom-right corner.

nodes.name: RenderBoardFrame
nodes.summary: Renders a named grouping frame on the canvas: a semi-transparent coloured rectangle with a draggable header bar and resize grip, moving all contained cards/textboxes/pins when dragged.

nodes.name: BuildBoardCard
nodes.summary: Builds the visual card for a single world entity on the board canvas, with faction colour indicator, type badge, name, key fields, portrait thumbnail, and a resizable border.

nodes.name: RenderRelationVisuals
nodes.summary: Draws all visual elements for a board relation: the styled line segments (solid/dashed/dotted), waypoint bubbles, arrowhead polygon, caption badge, wide hit-zone lines for hover detection, and hover-highlight logic.

nodes.name: AutoArrangeBoard
nodes.summary: Automatically arranges entity cards on the board in a grid layout, optionally restricting to a supplied set of selected entity IDs. Persists the new positions via EntityBoardService.

nodes.name: HandleBoardConnectClick
nodes.summary: Handles entity card clicks during relation-connect mode: records the source entity on first click, then creates a new BoardRelation between source and target on second click, prompting the relation dialog.

nodes.name: WorldEntityEditDialog.cs
nodes.summary: Schema-driven modal WPF dialog for creating or editing world entities (Characters, Locations, Factions, Lore entries). Character editing shows a wider window with a portrait drag-drop zone; each entity type renders its schema fields plus type-specific extras such as faction colour picker, member chip lists, and reference image attachment.

nodes.name: Show
nodes.summary: Static entry point: creates and shows the dialog modally, sets an editOpenFlag guard to prevent re-entrant opens, and returns true if the user saved.

nodes.name: BuildPortraitZone
nodes.summary: Builds the portrait drag-drop zone for Character entities: shows a placeholder or the loaded image, supports file-picker and drop, offers crop and remove overlay buttons, and auto-offers the crop dialog when the image proportions or size are unsuitable.

nodes.name: BuildImageAttachmentZone
nodes.summary: Builds a reference image attachment zone for Location and Faction entities: supports drag-drop and file picker, shows a thumbnail with Open/Open-with/Remove actions and a context menu.

nodes.name: BuildFactionExtras
nodes.summary: Appends Faction-specific UI sections to the editor: a colour swatch picker from the shared palette and chip-panel member lists for both Character and Location member types.

nodes.name: BuildMemberSection
nodes.summary: Builds a generic chip-panel section listing members of a given entity type, with add and remove interactions, used for both Character/Location membership in Factions and character knowledge in Lore entries.

nodes.name: BuildLocationFactionSection
nodes.summary: Builds a faction membership chip panel for Location and Lore entities, displaying each faction's colour dot alongside its name and offering add/remove interactions.

nodes.name: GoogleAIService
nodes.summary: Implements ICloudAIService for Google's Generative Language API (Gemini), translating ClaudetRelay's message format into Google's contents/parts schema and stripping thinking tokens from Gemini Flash Thinking responses.

layers.name: Application Entry & Build Config
layers.description: WPF application bootstrap (App.xaml/cs), assembly metadata, MSBuild project and solution files, and repository-level config files that define how ClaudetRelay is compiled and launched.

layers.name: UI / Presentation Layer
layers.description: All WPF windows, dialogs, and UI utilities that form the user-facing surface of ClaudetRelay — including the massive MainWindow orchestration hub, Settings, Participants, World Builder UI, and image crop dialogs.

layers.name: AI Provider Integrations
layers.description: The ICloudAIService interface and all provider implementations (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, OpenRouter, xAI Grok, Ollama) together with the rate limiter and self-description generator that abstract away each external AI API.

layers.name: Multi-Model Orchestration
layers.description: The MCP server that exposes ClaudetRelay participants as external tools and the Bridge-mode controller that routes messages between a directing model and participant models in automated multi-agent workflows.

layers.name: Application Services
layers.description: Core application services for project and settings persistence, chat log management, roadmap tracking, World Builder entity data, export (HTML/Markdown), thumbnail generation, Windows Credential Manager API key storage, and OXSUIT theme file loading.

layers.name: Theme Library
layers.description: 101 OXSUIT theme definition files (XML) covering a wide range of visual styles — from accessibility high-contrast themes to franchise-inspired palettes (Warhammer 40K, Fallout, Star Citizen, anime) — loaded at runtime by OxsuitLoader.

layers.name: Project Templates & Documentation
layers.description: XAML UI layout templates for each supported project type (Business, Game, Novel, etc.) together with their bundled seed plan documents (design docs, character sheets, task trackers) that are copied into new projects, plus the top-level README.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the best starting point for understanding what ClaudetRelay is: a Windows desktop workspace that lets up to 20 AI participants from eight different providers collaborate in a single conversation. It documents the five major feature pillars — multi-agent chat, the project system, the World Builder, MCP/Bridge mode, and the 101-theme OXSUIT library — giving you the mental map you need before reading any code.

tour.title: Build Config & Target Framework
tour.description: ClaudetRelay.csproj declares the project as a .NET 10 WPF application, lists every NuGet dependency (Anthropic SDK, OpenAI SDK, Markdig, etc.), and controls how resources like theme files and project templates are bundled into the output. Reading this file tells you which external libraries are in play and confirms the Windows-only WPF target before you look at any C# code.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: App.xaml and App.xaml.cs form the WPF bootstrap pair. The XAML file declares the App class and sets the startup URI to MainWindow, while the code-behind enforces single-instance behavior (so only one copy of ClaudetRelay runs at a time), registers global exception handlers, and triggers the first window creation. This is where the process begins before any AI provider or UI logic is touched.

tour.title: Main Window — The Central Hub
tour.description: MainWindow.xaml defines the entire tabbed shell — Chat, Projects, Roadmap, World Builder, and Settings tabs — while MainWindow.xaml.cs at 18,800 lines is the monolithic controller that wires everything together. It holds references to every service, dispatches messages to the right AI provider for each participant, manages conversation history, and coordinates the UI state across all features. Understanding this file's structure is the key to understanding how all the other components connect.

tour.title: AI Provider Interface
tour.description: ICloudAIService.cs defines the contract that every AI provider must implement — methods for sending messages, handling streaming responses, and reporting model capabilities. With the highest fan-in score in the codebase (referenced by three provider implementations), this interface is the pivot point that lets MainWindow.xaml.cs treat Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Ollama identically when dispatching participant turns.

tour.title: Anthropic & OpenAI-Compatible Providers
tour.description: AnthropicService.cs implements ICloudAIService for Claude models, handling streaming responses and tool-use callbacks through the Anthropic SDK. OpenAICompatibleService.cs is a base class that implements the same interface for the OpenAI chat-completions API format, and serves as the parent for OpenAI, Groq, Mistral, OpenRouter, xAI Grok, and OllamaOpenAI — all providers that share the same wire protocol. Together these two files cover the majority of supported providers through inheritance rather than duplication.

tour.title: Google & Ollama Providers
tour.description: GoogleAIService.cs implements ICloudAIService for the Gemini family, which uses Google's own REST format rather than the OpenAI wire protocol, so it cannot extend OpenAICompatibleService and must implement the interface directly. OllamaService.cs handles the Ollama native API for locally-hosted models and adds special support for thinking/reasoning tokens that Ollama exposes but the OpenAI-compatible shim does not. Together these two providers demonstrate the two implementation paths in the provider hierarchy.

tour.title: Rate Limiting & Secure Key Storage
tour.description: ProviderRateLimiter.cs enforces per-provider requests-per-minute limits, queuing calls and releasing them on a token-bucket schedule so the app never triggers API throttling errors. WindowsCredentialManager.cs stores and retrieves API keys exclusively through the Windows Credential Manager (DPAPI), which means keys are encrypted by the OS to the current user account and never written to disk in plaintext. Both services are foundational to every provider call dispatched by MainWindow.

tour.title: Settings & Participants Configuration
tour.description: SettingsService.cs persists application-wide configuration — provider endpoints, model selections, orchestration mode, and UI preferences — as JSON in AppData. SettingsWindow.xaml.cs and ParticipantsWindow.cs are the UI dialogs that surface those settings to the user, letting them assign providers, write personas, set roles (Coordinator or Reasoner), and configure character files for each of the up to 20 participants.

tour.title: MCP Server & Bridge Mode
tour.description: McpServer.cs exposes ClaudetRelay's participants as tools through the Model Context Protocol, allowing an external AI (like Claude Code) to call individual participants by name as MCP tool invocations. ModelControllerRunner.cs implements Bridge mode, where a designated controller model routes messages to and from participant models, enabling structured multi-model pipelines. McpServer calls ModelControllerRunner when it needs to orchestrate a full routing cycle, making these two files the gateway between ClaudetRelay and the broader AI ecosystem.

tour.title: Project System
tour.description: ProjectService.cs is the filesystem API for the project system: it handles creating, loading, and deleting projects, persisting and replaying chat history per project, taking ZIP backups, and running sandboxed file read/write operations that AI agents request during a session. ProjectTypeDefinition.cs loads the schema that describes each project type (Novel, Software, Theatre, etc.) from the ProjectTypes/ directory, and RoadmapService.cs manages milestone and progress data stored alongside the project.

tour.title: Project Type Templates
tour.description: The ProjectTypes/ directory contains XAML files that define the UI layout for each project type's dedicated panel, plus seed documents that are copied into a new project folder on creation. General.xaml is the default catch-all template, while SoftwareProject.xaml shows how a richer type adds specialized fields and structure. These templates are the bridge between the abstract project-type schema loaded by ProjectTypeDefinition.cs and the concrete tabs the user sees in MainWindow.

tour.title: World Builder Feature
tour.description: The World Builder is a creative-writing and game-design tool built as a self-contained subsystem. MainWindow.World.cs is a partial class extension of MainWindow that handles entity management and faction UI within the main window's tab. WorldBoardWindow.cs (4,353 lines) implements the free-canvas board where entity cards can be dragged, connected with named relations, and auto-arranged. WorldEntityEditDialog.cs provides a schema-driven form for creating and editing Characters, Locations, Factions, and Lore entries, while WorldEntityService.cs (depth 3 in the BFS) persists all of this data to disk alongside the active project.

tour.title: Export & OXSUIT Theme System
tour.description: ExportService.cs renders the active conversation to HTML or Markdown, applying syntax highlighting and participant labels so the output is readable outside the app. OxsuitLoader.cs reads plain XML .oxsuit files from the Themes/ folder at runtime and maps their color tokens into WPF resource dictionaries, enabling hot-reload of any theme without restarting. The two sample OXSUIT files (Dracula.oxsuit and CatppuccinMocha.oxsuit) illustrate the simple key-value XML format that backs all 101 built-in themes.
